title: Windo Nend
content:
{{Top|ffa|leg}} {{Update|[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]}} {{Character |type=Jedi |image=[[File:Warblenend.jpg]] |name=Windo "Warble" Nend |homeworld=[[Ando/Legends|Ando]][[Republic 57|''Republic'' 57]] |birth= |death=[[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]],''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]''{{TCW|Heroes on Both Sides}} [[Jabiim/Legends|Jabiim]] |species=[[Aqualish/Legends|Aqualish]] {{C|[[Quara/Legends|Quara]]}}[[Republic 56|''Republic'' 56]] |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]] |pronouns=new |height= |mass= |hair=[[Color/Legends|Black]] |feathers= |eyes=Black |skin= |cyber= |families=new |parents=new |partners=new |siblings=new |children=new |affiliation=*[[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] *[[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|I'm staying too. This is what my master would have wanted. It's what all of our masters would have wanted.|Windo Nend|}} '''Windo Nend''', called "'''Warble'''" by his friends, was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Aqualish/Legends|Quara Aqualish]] [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] and [[Jedi Commander/Legends|Jedi Commander]] during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]]. In 21 BBY, he was stationed on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Jabiim/Legends|Jabiim]], where [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic]] and [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] forces vied with the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Separatist]] contingent under [[General/Legends|General]] [[Alto Stratus]]. Nend, having lost his [[Jedi Master/Legends|master]] in the war, was part of a group of similar Jedi students known as the [[Padawan Pack]]; the theory of the Jedi leadership on the planet was that a group of Padawans acting in concert would have the combined abilities of a smaller number of [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knights]] or Masters. As the rest of the Jedi on the planet were killed off, Nend and the Padawan Pack stood with the only line of defense against Separatist reinforcements as the Republic forces attempted to evacuate. On the forty-first day of the [[Battle of Jabiim|battle]], Nend was killed when a Separatist [[IG-227 Hailfire-class droid tank/Legends|Hailfire]] [[Droid/Legends|droid]] rolled over him, but not before he could set off explosives to disable the droid. Later, his colleague and fellow Padawan, [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]], whispered Nend's name to a [[Memory moth/Legends|memory moth]] in a [[Jedi monument (New Holstice)|Jedi monument]] on [[New Holstice/Legends|New Holstice]], to be repeated alongside the names of other fallen Jedi for eternity. ==Biography== ===Battle of Jabiim=== Windo "Warble" Nend, a [[Aqualish/Legends|Quara Aqualish]] [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]], had lost his [[Jedi Master/Legends|master]] in the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], when Nend was assigned to [[Shelter Base]] on the contested [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Jabiim/Legends|Jabiim]]. The planet had been a member of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] until the outbreak of the Clone Wars, but had suffered much due to a lack of Republic response to local problems. A vocal [[Jabiimi]] leader known as [[Alto Stratus]] spoke out against the Republic and the Jedi, and Jabiim seceded. But before Stratus could make a deal with [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]] leader [[Count/Legends|Count]] [[Dooku/Legends|Dooku]], in which he promised the Separatists Jabiim's rich supplies of [[Ore/Legends|ore]] in exchange for military hardware and support, the [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army of the Republic]] landed on the planet. Stratus and his [[Nimbus commando]]s, backed up by units from the [[Separatist Droid Army/Legends|Separatist Droid Army]], were locked into battle with the Jedi-led Republic [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone]] forces.[[Republic 55|''Republic'' 55]] ===A pack of Padawans=== {{Quote|Warble, if you talk the entire trip, I swear I'm going to use my rusty fingers to rip out your tusks.|Zule Xiss|}} Several master-less Padawans were organized by the Jedi leadership on Jabiim into one unit, known colloquially as the [[Padawan Pack]]. The theory behind this was that a large group of Padawans would possess in their combined talents the same amount of resources as a smaller group of [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knights]] and Masters. However, some, including [[Advanced Recon Commando/Legends|ARC trooper]] [[Alpha (Advanced Recon Commando)|Alpha-17]], were of the opinion that this group posed more problems than they solved. [[File:Padawanpack.jpg|thumb|left|270px|Nend with other members of the Padawan Pack]] Nend was present in the Padawan Pack on the thirty-second day of the [[Battle of Jabiim|battle]] after it had been joined by [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]], a Padawan whose master, [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]], was presumed [[Death/Legends|dead]]. At this point, the Pack included, in addition to Nend and Skywalker, the Padawans [[Tae Diath]], [[Mak Lotor]], [[Elora Sund]], [[Kass Tod]], [[Vaabesh]], [[Aubrie Wyn]], and [[Zule Xiss]]. [[Jedi General/Legends|Jedi General]] [[Leska]], the senior Jedi on the planet at that time, had ordered the group to guard the resupply lines, as she was loath to send more young Jedi to their deaths. As they guarded a supply convoy including [[All Terrain Armored Transport/Legends|AT-AT]] and [[All Terrain Experimental Transport|AT-XT]] [[Walker/Legends|walkers]] heading for the Republic stronghold of [[Cobalt Station]], Nend and Aubrie Wyn, a [[Human/Legends|Human]] female, stayed with the vehicles while Skywalker led the rest of the Padawans and a group of [[Cloning/Legends|clones]] to intercept and destroy [[Droid/Legends|droid]] forces advancing upon the convoy. Later, Nend attempted to make conversation with the female [[Falleen/Legends|Falleen]] Zule Xiss, only to be met with Xiss's frustration over their situation; the [[Gand/Legends|Gand]] male Vaabesh helped Nend to understand Xiss's history and reasons for defensiveness—a Padawan who had bounced from Master to Master, Xiss had seen her final and most effective mentor killed in the same battle in which she had been previously maimed. Two days later, the convoy came under attack once again. An AT-XT walker unexpectedly activated a [[Mine/Legends|land mine]], in the first Separatist use of mines known to the Republic in the Clone Wars. The explosion caught Nend off-guard, throwing him from his feet. The female [[Zabrak/Legends|Zabrak]] Padawan, Kass Tod, helped Nend up just as the convoy was assaulted by the Nimbus commandos, led in person by Stratus himself. Although surrounded by Stratus's forces and a field of Separatist mines, the Padawans were able to use their assortment of skills to lead the convoy out of the fray; Nend took the point, warning the others to watch for mines. His attempt to stop the convoy and kill the Jedi thwarted, Stratus ordered his forces to reorganize for an assault on Cobalt Station itself. ===A last stand=== {{Quote|There is no fear, there is no pain, there is no death—''"
"''There is only the Force.|Vaabesh and Windo Nend, paraphrasing the Jedi Code|}} On the thirty-seventh day of the battle, the Padawan Pack reached Cobalt Station, one of two Republic strongholds left on Jabiim. Jedi [[General/Legends|General]] Leska had been killed earlier that day as she completed operations against Separatist forces on the planet's [[Razor Coast]]. The day saw a break in the storms that had plagued Jabiim for thirty-seven days, preventing the Republic forces from leaving the planet—and Separatist reinforcements from landing. As the break occurred, Vaabesh spotted an armada of Separatist [[C-9979 landing craft/Legends|C-9979 landing craft]] descending from the sky, bearing a fresh army at Stratus's disposal. The Padawan Pack, last of the Jedi left on Jabiim, and their forces took refuge in Cobalt Station, where they would be forced to wait until another break in the storms for an ordered Republic evacuation to begin. [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Gillmunn]], a Jabiimi loyalist, held the remaining Republic and loyalist forces on a [[Monsoon Mesa|mesa]] south of the station. [[File:WindoNendDeath.jpg|thumb|right|270px|The death of Windo "Warble" Nend]] At that point, the Padawans were faced with a decision. They could stay at Cobalt Station, holding off Stratus's army of over 10,000 Nimbus commandos and [[Battle droid/Legends|battle droids]], buying time for the Republic forces to evacuate, or they could flee to the mesa and hope to evacuate before the enemy forces arrived. Skywalker and Mak Lotor, a Human male, pointed out that their holding off of the Separatist units was the mission's last hope, and so all of the Padawans, including Nend, decided to stay at Cobalt Station. As they prepared for the coming battle, Nend strapped [[Grenade/Legends|grenades]] to himself as additional weapons to his [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]]. On the forty-first day of the battle, Nend found himself face-to-face with the Separatist army. As he fought, he uttered assurances to himself, based on the [[Jedi Code/Legends|Jedi Code]]. After being blasted backward by enemy fire, Nend noticed a [[IG-227 Hailfire-class droid tank/Legends|Hailfire droid]] bearing down on him. Before it rolled over the Aqualish, killing him, Nend managed to trigger one of the explosives on his chest, vaporizing himself and disabling the droid. ===Legacy=== {{Quote|There is no death…|Windo Nend, immediately before sacrificing himself|}} [[File:Memorymoths.jpg|thumb|left|220px|The monument to Nend and other fallen Jedi on New Holstice]] The Battle of Jabiim continued for two more days. The members of the Padawan Pack died one by one in defense of Cobalt Station, with Aubrie Wyn killing General Stratus before her own death, save Anakin Skywalker, who rode a [[Swoop/Legends|swoop]] to the mesa evacuation point. Upon arrival, simultaneous with that of the Republic ships, Skywalker was forced to choose which forces would evacuate; he grudgingly chose his clone forces over the Jabiimi loyalists, thus earning the ire of Gillmunn and his men, but saving the clones to fight on other fronts. With Republic presence nonexistent, the battle for Jabiim was ultimately lost, but Windo Nend and the Padawan Pack managed to prevent the rout and deaths of many more.[[Republic 58|''Republic'' 58]] Days later, on the Republic world of [[New Holstice/Legends|New Holstice]], Skywalker added [[Memory moth/Legends|memory moths]] containing the names of all his fallen comrades from the Padawan Pack, including Windo "Warble" Nend, to a [[Jedi monument (New Holstice)|monument]] set up by the residents of that planet for all Jedi that had been killed in service to the Republic. There, Nend's name would be continually whispered by the moth's wings for eternity. ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|There is no fatigue…|Windo Nend, in the heat of battle|}} Nend, a Quara Aqualish, had greenish skin. He was quite talkative, trying to make conversation with his comrades, much to the ire of the distraught and jaded Zule Xiss. However, when apprised of Xiss's situation, Nend was sympathetic and compassionate. As he prepared for and fought in battle, Nend took comfort in the Jedi Code and its interpretations, uttering them as did his colleague, Vaabesh. He fought with a blue-bladed lightsaber, though in the last stand at Cobalt Station he strapped to himself eight grenades for additional firepower. Nend fought to the last, sacrificing himself to disable a powerful enemy headed for his friends' location. ==Behind the scenes== Windo Nend's appearances all fall within the [[Star Wars: Clone Wars Volume 3: Last Stand on Jabiim|''Last Stand on Jabiim'']] story arc of the ''[[Star Wars: Republic]]'' [[Star Wars comic books|comic books]], which was written by [[W. Haden Blackman|Haden Blackman]] and drawn by [[Brian Ching]]. Although the Padawan Pack was first mentioned in [[Republic 55|''Republic'' 55]], Nend himself did not appear with the unit until the [[Republic 56|next issue]]. He died in the [[Republic 57|subsequent issue]], and finally was given a brief mention at the Jedi monument in [[Republic 58|''Republic'' 58]]. ==Appearances== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Windo Nend}} *[[Republic 56|''Republic'' 56]] {{1st}} *[[Republic 57|''Republic'' 57]] *[[Republic 58|''Republic'' 58]] {{Mo}} ==Sources== *''[[Star Wars: The Comics Companion]]'' *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Windo Nend |es=Windo Nend |pl=Windo Nend |deGA=1 }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nend, Windo}} [[Category:Aqualish]] [[Category:Jedi Commanders]] [[Category:Jedi trainees of the Jedi Order]] [[Category:Males]]
